activities_projects.title: Mock Election in Classroom
activities_projects.objective: Understand the process of elections and voting
steps: Select 3–4 students as candidates
Prepare ballot papers with candidate names
Students cast their votes secretly
Count the votes and declare the winner
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how voting and counting take place in elections
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and correct understanding of election process (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Election Campaign Poster
activities_projects.objective: Learn how political parties communicate with voters
steps: Create a political party name
Design a poster with a slogan and symbol
Present the campaign poster to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the role of election campaigns
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and clarity of message (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Identify Election Symbols
activities_projects.objective: Understand the importance of election symbols
steps: Collect pictures of different party symbols
Write the party name beside each symbol
Discuss why symbols help voters
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn how symbols help voters identify parties

activities_projects.title: Create a Voters List
activities_projects.objective: Understand the concept of electoral roll
steps: List names of all students in the class
Assign them voter ID numbers
Prepare a simple voters list
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how voters lists are prepared
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and completeness of list (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Debate on Importance of Elections
activities_projects.objective: Understand why elections are important in democracy
steps: Divide students into two groups
Discuss why elections are necessary
Present arguments during a class debate
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the role of elections in democracy
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of arguments and participation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Election Process Flowchart
activities_projects.objective: Learn the stages of the election process
steps: List the stages of elections such as nomination, campaign, polling, and counting
Draw a flowchart showing these steps
Explain each stage briefly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the full election procedure

activities_projects.title: Role of Election Commission Chart
activities_projects.objective: Understand the functions of the Election Commission
steps: Write the functions of the Election Commission
Illustrate them with small examples
Display the chart in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize the importance of an independent Election Commission

activities_projects.title: Interview a Voter
steps: Interview a family member who has voted
Ask about their voting experience
Record their answers in a notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how citizens participate in elections

activities_projects.title: Election Slogan Writing
steps: Think of an issue important to people
Write a short election slogan
Share the slogan with the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn how slogans are used in campaigns

activities_projects.title: Identify Fair and Unfair Election Practices
steps: List examples such as bribing voters or fair campaigning
Classify them as fair or unfair practices
Discuss why fairness is important
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the importance of free and fair elections
